title: calophyllum woodii
intro: Calophyllum woodii, also known as the White Bead Tree, is a tropical tree species native to Southeast Asia, characterized by its distinctive white, bead-like flowers and yellowish-brown wood. The wood of the Calophyllum woodii plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Calophyllum woodii include its ability to reduce inflammation, treat wounds, and prevent infections, making it a valuable resource for natural remedies. Calophyllum woodii can be prepared into various herbal forms, such as essential oils, extracts, and timbers, which can be used for medicinal purposes.
